content.title: Lecanemab in Early Alzheimer’s Disease: Clarity AD Study
content.abstract: Background: Alzheimer's disease is characterized by amyloid-beta deposition... Methods: In this phase 3, double-blind trial, we randomly assigned patients with early Alzheimer's disease... Results: At 18 months, lecanemab reduced brain amyloid levels by 93%...
conclusions: Lecanemab reduced markers of amyloid in early Alzheimer's disease
Cognitive decline was 27% slower in the treatment group compared to placebo
Adverse events included infusion reactions and ARIA
Benefit-risk profile supports use in patients with early Alzheimer's disease

key_findings.finding: Primary endpoint CDR-SB score difference of -0.45 (p<0.001)
Amyloid reduction by 93% at 18 months measured by PET
ARIA-E occurred in 12.6% of treatment group vs 1.7% placebo

figure_2.label: Figure 2
figure_2.caption: Change from baseline in CDR-SB score
